parts.displayText: Hello there I'm Māris Ločmelis and this is page about my self

contents.content: I was born in 1987. My hobbies are related to PCs (playing games, watching anime, listening to educational videos).
Love results, hate processes. If a process can be skipped, I will skip it for sure.
I like talking — you may ask me to stop and I will stop.
I hate reading books but love reading code.
Don't like to give orders prefer to ask favors.
love to improve everything, You are included.
Love results, hate processes. If there is a way to get results without process, I will definitely find it.
I'm a lion person (like a cat one but with a loud roar).
Like to move by small steps, don't like rushing.
Like to get maximum out of what I have, won't search for something new if I can use the existing one.
Hate spaghetti. Love SOLID, work LoD
Hate to do everything by myself. We are who we are due to uniting, not because someone had big biceps.
Brains are a muscle. If you need them, you must train them.
Don't say 'Maris, I'm okay with it' if it's not true. I will eat you like an ice-cream (with taste).
Love predictable risk, hate risks that are not calculated.
If I fall, I will jump right back. Anything that doesn't kill me makes me stronger.
Don't be scared that you will harm my feelings.
I'm a mirror — I throw back everything you throw at me (it will fly even if I won't be able to lift it).
Try harder is good, but think smarter is better!
If you ask me to do something in the middle of the night, it will be done. But I will expect that we’ll work to make sure it never happens again.
I believe that valuable things need time. If you make something fast, why do you expect it to live another day without a problem?
If I can calculate mistake at the end, I won't do it — as the result is fully predictable.
Lazy bastard making the maximum out of doing nothing.
My ideas are unique. If you think they are not, then you just never discussed anything with me.
I respect all people, no matter their status, skills, or any other thing that seems important for most people.
Every recommendation you give, try to follow it yourself.
Make code more readable (Clean Code, DRY).
Don't expect others to do something you wouldn't do yourself.
Before changing anything, try creating something new instead.
Gather suggestions and do something if they are to many.
If you'd like to change something, think twice — sometimes changing your mind about the problem is easier and better.
Don't take new tools until you've mastered the existing ones.
don't rush time, or time will break you down.
when you see something think how some one sees this in they're eyes.
don't solve imagined problems use imagination to create something new.
don't break something, before you make something to take it's place.
Use tools but doesn't allow tools to own you.
The more turns you make the worth path you have chosen.
SOLID — even if you can't fully apply it, aim for the closest option.
Try to follow KISS, but if it's impossible, keep it complex only when necessary.
YARGNI — use it always and you'll find some free time.
Clean Code doesn't make your code better, faster or simpler. It makes your code more readable!
Humans should work with humans, not on creating code that no one understands!
Don't take dogs leg and move forward, learn how properly to give command.
